figures.caption: Figure 1. Overview of the proposed synergistic prompt and prototype learning framework. The framework is trained in two stages: Stage-1 freezes both the image and text encoders, and aligns unlabeled day/night images with learnable textual prompts via instance-aware dynamicbias adaptation. Stage-2 activates the image encoder and incorporates: (i) intra-domain identity association using prototype memory banks to enhance feature discriminability within each domain, and (ii) cross-domain prototype matching to identify reliable positive and negative prototype pairs, establishing annotation-free identity correspondences across day and night domains.
Figure 2. Illustration of the Cross-Domain Prototype Matching.

Table 1. Performance comparison with state-of-the-art methods on supervised and unsupervised tasks. FSL and USL denote fullysupervised and unsupervised learning methods, respectively. Bold and underline indicate the best and second-best results.

Table 2. Zero-shot generalization performance of unsupervised methods. “DN-Wild→DN-348” indicates training on DN-Wild in an unsupervised manner and directly evaluating on DN-348 without fine-tuning.“DN-348→DN-Wild” denotes the reverse.
Figure 4. Cosine similarity distributions of positive and negative day–night pairs on the DN-348 dataset. δ denotes the margin between the mean similarities of positive and negative pairs.
Figure 6. Performance comparison of clustering metrics against state-of-the-art methods on DN-348.
Figure 7. Comparative visualization of Grad-CAM activation maps for the baseline model and the proposed method.

Figure 8. A comparison of the top-5 retrieval results under cross-domain day-night conditions is presented for the proposed method and the baseline. A green bounding box indicates a match with the ground truth identity, whereas a red box denotes a mismatch.
